---
title: "CV"
---

<a class="lang-switch" href="/zh/cv.html">中文</a>

<section class="panel panel-light">
<p>I am trained in human geography, with research interests in scientific mobility, regional innovation, urban networks, and empirical work using longitudinal relational data. This page is a short public overview; the PDF contains the full public CV.</p>
<h2>Curriculum Vitae</h2>
<p><a class="btn btn-primary" href="assets/cv/Ran_Wang_CV_Public.pdf">Download CV</a></p>
</section>

<section class="panel panel-muted">
<h2>Education</h2>
<p><strong>East China Normal University.</strong> M.Sc. in Human Geography, 2023-2026. School of Geographic Sciences; economic geography and regional innovation.</p>
<p><strong>Northeast Normal University.</strong> B.Sc. in Human Geography and Urban-Rural Planning, 2019-2023. Training in quantitative geography, GIS applications, urban geography, regional analysis, and planning.</p>
</section>

<section class="panel panel-light">
<h2>Research Outputs</h2>
<ul>
<li><strong>Spatial Structure and Determinants of Top Global Biomedical Scientists' Mobility from an Urban Network Perspective</strong> — accepted article.</li>
<li><strong>Intercity Mobility Network of Top Global Biomedical Scientists: A Dual Perspective of Whole and Ego Networks</strong> — master's thesis.</li>
<li><strong>Author-Institution Linkage and Geocoding Feasibility Audit</strong> — public code repository.</li>
</ul>
</section>

<section class="panel panel-dark">
<h2>Methods and Skills</h2>
<p>Python; R; Stata; GIS; social network analysis; ego-network analysis; conditional logit models; ERGM/WERGM; entity resolution; longitudinal multi-source data construction; coverage audits.</p>
</section>
